zh.lifeSummary: 當Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim於1183年出生在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德布洛可斯特時，他的父親Willem I van Bronchorst29歲，母親Gertrud von Ahaus25歲。他於1225年與Kunigonde von Oldenburg結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1241年2月27日在他的家鄉享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim于1183年出生在荷兰蓋爾德藍德布洛可斯特时，他的父亲Willem I van Bronchorst29岁，母亲Gertrud von Ahaus25岁。他于1225年与Kunigonde von Oldenburg结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1241年2月27日在他的家乡享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Bronchorst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: habitational name from a small town called Bronkhorst by the river Ijssel near Steenderen in Gelderland province. The placename derives from bronk see Bronk + horst ‘higher located brushwood’.

variants.name: Brunkhorst
meanings: North German: habitational name from a place called Brunkhorst, a cognate of the Dutch name   Bronkhorst  .

variants.name: Bronk
meanings: Dutch: nickname for someone known for sulking or flaunting behavior, from bronken (brunken, perhaps also pronken) ‘to sulk, to strut, to flaunt’.
Dutch: from the personal name Bronne, a variant of Bruno + the diminutive suffix -ke.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from the village of Bronki, in eastern Poland.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim was born in 1183, in Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Netherlands, his father, Willem I van Bronchorst, was 29 and his mother, Gertrud von Ahaus, was 25. He married Kunigonde von Oldenburg in 1225. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 27 February 1241, in his hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim est né en 1183, à Bronckhorst, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, son père, Willem I van Bronchorst, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gertrud von Ahaus, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Kunigonde von Oldenburg en 1225. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 27 février 1241, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim 1183 in Bronckhorst, Geldern, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Willem I van Bronchorst 29 und seine Mutter Gertrud von Ahaus 25. Er hat 1225 Kunigonde von Oldenburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 27. Februar 1241 im Alter von 58 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim è nato il nel 1183 nel Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Willem I van Bronchorst, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Gertrud von Ahaus, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Kunigonde von Oldenburg nel 1225. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 27 febbraio 1241, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheimが1183年にオランダジェルダーランドブロンックhオルストで生まれた時，父Willem I van Bronchorstは29歳，母Gertrud von Ahausは25歳であった。1225年にKunigonde von Oldenburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1241年2月27日に故郷で58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim(이)가 1183년 네덜란드 글델랜드 브론크호르스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Willem I van Bronchorst(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertrud von Ahaus(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1225년 Kunigonde von Oldenburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1241년 2월 27일 그의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim nasceu em 1183, em Bronckhorst, Gheldria, Países Baixos, seu pai, Willem I van Bronchorst, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Gertrud von Ahaus, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Kunigonde von Oldenburg em 1225. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 27 de fevereiro de 1241, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim родился в 1183 г. в местности бронккхаурст, джелдирланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Willem I van Bronchorst было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertrud von Ahaus, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Kunigonde von Oldenburg в 1225 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 27 февраля 1241 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gijsbert II van Bronchorst - Rekheim nació en 1183, en Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Países Bajos, su papá, Willem I van Bronchorst, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Gertrud von Ahaus, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Kunigonde von Oldenburg en 1225. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 27 de febrero de 1241, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años.
